titles.text: ちびまる子ちゃん ChibiMaruko Chan 「はりきり365日」の巻
ちびまる子ちゃん 「はりきり365日」の巻

descriptions.text:
ちびまる子ちゃん：はりきり365日の巻は、ちびまる子ちゃんの「スライス・オブ・ライフ」マンガを基にしたバーチャルボードゲームです。ちびまる子ちゃんは、このゲームの前後にいくつかのビデオゲームに登場していますが、スーパーファミコンシリーズの他のタイトルは「ちびまる子ちゃん：めざせ！南の島！」だけです。

ゲームプレイの目的は、28〜31のマスで構成されたボードを進みながら、役立つアイテムを見つけ、サイコロを振った後に得られるものによってお金を増やしたり減らしたりすることです。最初にゴールを通過したプレイヤーには100円のボーナスが与えられますが、低い数字を続けて出すプレイヤーは、特に運が良ければ長期的にもっとお金を持つことになるかもしれません。ゲームは1ヶ月から12ヶ月まで設定でき、最終的な勝者は獲得したお金の合計によって決まります。

Chibi Maruko-chan (Japanese: ちびまる子ちゃん; lit. "Little Maruko-chan") is a manga series written and illustrated by Momoko Sakura. The series depicts the simple, everyday life of Momoko Sakura, a young girl everyone calls Maruko, and her family in suburban Japan in the year 1974. Maruko is a troublemaker, and every episode recounts Maruko's trouble and how she and her friends succeed in resolving the situation.
The series is set in the former Irie District (入江町), Shimizu, now part of Shizuoka City, birthplace of its author.
The first story under the title "Chibi Maruko-chan" was published in the August 1986 edition of the manga magazine Ribon. Other semi-autobiographical stories by the author had appeared in Ribon and Ribon Original in 1984 and 1985, and were included in the first "Chibi Maruko-chan" tankōbon in 1987. The author first began writing and submitting strips in her final year of senior high school, although Shueisha (the publisher of Ribon and Ribon Original) did not decide to run them until over a year later. The author's intent was to write "essays in manga form"; many stories are inspired by incidents from her own life, and some characters are based on her family and frie
